student_verification.name: student
student_verification.declaration: I used Claude to help design and implement the two ablations. I chose Polar POS and Twitter-specific features as the two components based on my reading of Table 6 and Section 8.1.2. The interpretations connect back to my own assumptions from Task 1.2.

interactions.prompt: got it now let's move to task 3.1
interactions.response_summary: Proposed two ablation candidates: removing Polar POS features (f1, f8) and removing Twitter-specific features (f3, f4, f6), with rationale for each based on Table 6 and Section 8.1.2.

interactions.prompt: why these two specifically and not like removing negation or the last third window
interactions.response_summary: Explained that f1/f8 are the most important features per Table 6 so removing them gives the most interpretable result, and Twitter-specific features are explicitly called 'marginal' in Section 8.1.2 — so both ablations test a specific paper claim rather than arbitrary removal.

interactions.prompt: ohk makes sense...so how do we actually implement it without rewriting everything
interactions.response_summary: Suggested a parametrised extract_features() with two boolean flags — use_polar_pos and use_twitter_specific — so ablated versions are just one flag flip away from the full model.

interactions.prompt: ok can you now write the full notebook for this with both ablations and the plots
interactions.response_summary: Built the full task_3_1 notebook with the parametrised pipeline, evaluation for full model + both ablations, two plots per ablation (bar chart + per-fold line), and 5-7 sentence interpretations with section references.
interactions.student_modification: Verified that flipping use_polar_pos=False does not affect the Twitter-specific feature counts and vice versa.

interactions.prompt: for the interpretation part can you also connect it back to the assumptions from task 1.2
interactions.response_summary: Updated both interpretation cells to explicitly reference Assumption 1 (Twitter-specific) and Assumption 2 (Polar POS) from Task 1.2.

interactions.prompt: also add a summary table at the end comparing all three — full, ablation 1, ablation 2
interactions.response_summary: Added a markdown summary table at the end of the notebook with all three model rows and a key takeaway note.
